pi_items.source: Verified by reading setup_task.sh and export_result.sh, which write and read the broken plan from this exact path.
Verified by reading export_result.sh, which checks for the repaired mission file at this exact path.
Verified by reading export_result.sh, which writes the evaluation result to this file path.
Verified by reading verifier.py (which expects an altitude between 40m and 60m) and the task description instructions.
Verified by reading verifier.py, which checks altitude against ALT_LOW=40.0 and ALT_HIGH=60.0 (+/- 10m).
Verified by analyzing setup_task.sh, which intentionally modifies items[1], items[3], and items[4] with dangerous altitude errors.
Verified against MAVLink documentation and verifier.py RTL_COMMAND=20.

privileged_info_summary: The task accurately reports the target and broken mission file paths. The underlying data is a synthetic QGC mission plan at the standard PX4 SITL location (Zurich). Altitude errors are deliberately injected into mission items at indices 1, 3, and 4 via setup_task.sh. The expected altitude for correction is 50m with a 10m tolerance (40m-60m bounds enforced by verifier), and the agent must correctly append an RTL command (MAV_CMD=20), all of which explicitly match the metadata claims.
